description: Post-review fixes for the multi-tenant SaaS migration. Fix crashes, cross-tenant security holes, broken UI, and missing validations identified during code review.

userStories.title: Fix admin-promote crash: missing org_id on user creation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want admin-promote to work after the multi-tenant migration so that bootstrap admin creation does not crash.
acceptanceCriteria: In app/routers/auth.py, the admin-promote endpoint's new user insert dict includes org_id set to the Lever Money UUID '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001'
The new user insert dict also includes email set to req.username (so the user can log in via the email-based login flow)
When promoting an existing user (user already exists path), the logic remains unchanged
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The insert is at app/routers/auth.py around line 368-375. The new_user dict needs 'org_id': '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001' and 'email': req.username. The Lever Money UUID is the deterministic one from migration 008. Do NOT change the promote path (when user already exists) — only fix the create path.

userStories.title: Fix cross-org permission access in admin_users.py
userStories.description: As a platform owner, I want user permission endpoints to verify org membership so that admins cannot read or modify permissions for users in other organizations.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/admin/users/{user_id}/permissions fetches the target user and verifies target_user['org_id'] == user['org_id'] before returning data. Returns 404 if mismatch.
PUT /api/admin/users/{user_id}/permissions fetches the target user and verifies target_user['org_id'] == user['org_id'] before upserting. Returns 404 if mismatch.
Use the same pattern already used in update_user() and delete_user() in the same file (fetch target, compare org_id, raise 404)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In app/routers/admin_users.py: get_user_permissions is at ~line 141. It already fetches sellers by org_id (line 148-151), but does NOT verify the target user_id belongs to the same org. Add a check at the top: fetch user by id, verify org_id matches. Same for update_user_permissions at ~line 175 — line 185 checks user exists but does not check org_id. Follow the exact pattern from update_user (~line 99) and delete_user (~line 132) which already do this correctly.

userStories.title: Fix cross-org copy_logs update in copy.py
userStories.description: As a platform owner, I want copy log updates to be org-scoped so that one org's dimension retry cannot affect another org's log entries.
acceptanceCriteria: In copy_with_dims() endpoint: the copy_logs update that sets needs_dimensions status (around line 169-175) adds .eq('org_id', org_id) to the query
In retry_dimensions() endpoint: the final copy_logs update (around line 250-254) adds .eq('org_id', org_id) to the query
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In app/routers/copy.py: The first update is around line 169: db.table('copy_logs').update({...}).eq('source_item_id', item_id).eq('source_seller', req.source).eq('status', 'needs_dimensions').execute() — add .eq('org_id', org_id) before .execute(). The second update is around line 250: db.table('copy_logs').update({...}).eq('id', req.log_id).execute() — add .eq('org_id', org_id) before .execute(). The org_id variable is already available in both functions.

userStories.title: Fix OAuth install button blocked by popup blocker
userStories.description: As an org admin, I want the ML authorization button to work reliably so that I can connect Mercado Livre accounts without popup blockers interfering.
acceptanceCriteria: In Admin.tsx handleInstall(): replace window.open(data.redirect_url, '_blank') with window.location.href = data.redirect_url
Before redirecting, the function works the same: fetch with auth headers, parse JSON, handle errors with toast
If the fetch fails (401, network error), show toast error and do NOT redirect
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In frontend/src/pages/Admin.tsx, handleInstall() is at ~line 20. The only change needed is line 34: replace `window.open(data.redirect_url, '_blank')` with `window.location.href = data.redirect_url`. The rest of the function (fetch, error handling, loading state) stays the same. Do NOT add a confirmation dialog — just redirect directly like the old flow did.

userStories.title: Show specific signup error messages from backend
userStories.description: As a new user signing up, I want to see the specific reason my signup failed so that I can fix the problem and try again.
acceptanceCriteria: In useAuth.ts: signup() return type changes from Promise<boolean> to Promise<{success: boolean, error?: string}>
On API error, signup() parses the response JSON and returns {success: false, error: data.detail || 'Erro ao criar conta'}
On network error, signup() returns {success: false, error: 'Erro de conexao'}
On success, signup() returns {success: true}
In Signup.tsx: the handleSubmit reads the error field from signup() result and displays it
In Signup.tsx: add client-side validation before submit — if password.length < 6, show 'Senha deve ter pelo menos 6 caracteres' without calling the API
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In frontend/src/hooks/useAuth.ts: signup() is at ~line 84. Currently returns boolean. Change to: on !res.ok, parse error with `const data = await res.json().catch(() => null)` and return `{success: false, error: data?.detail || 'Erro ao criar conta'}`. On success return `{success: true}`. On catch return `{success: false, error: 'Erro de conexao'}`. In frontend/src/pages/Signup.tsx: handleSubmit at ~line 21 currently does `const success = await onSignup(...)`. Change to `const result = await onSignup(...)`. If !result.success, setError(result.error || 'Erro ao criar conta'). The onSignup prop type changes from `(e,p,c) => Promise<boolean>` to `(e,p,c) => Promise<{success: boolean, error?: string}>`. Add password length check before calling onSignup.

userStories.title: Protect debug endpoint with super-admin auth
userStories.description: As a platform owner, I want the debug environment endpoint to require authentication so that server configuration is not exposed publicly.
acceptanceCriteria: The /api/debug/env endpoint in app/main.py uses Depends(require_super_admin) as a parameter
Import require_super_admin from app.routers.auth
Unauthenticated requests get 401
Non-super-admin users get 403
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In app/main.py at ~line 56: change `async def debug_env():` to `async def debug_env(user: dict = Depends(require_super_admin)):`. Add imports at top: `from fastapi import Depends` (may already be imported) and `from app.routers.auth import require_super_admin`. The function body stays the same.
